amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Glycolysis is the central metabolic pathway converting glucose to pyruvate through 10 enzymatic steps, producing ATP and NADH. In yeast, this pathway is subject to allosteric regulation at key control points (hexokinase, phosphofructokinase, pyruvate kinase) and demonstrates metabolic logic through substrate availability checks and feedback regulation. The pathway represents one of evolution's most conserved biochemical programs, operating in virtually all organisms.
mermaid:
graph TD
    %% Input
    A[Extracellular Glucose] --> B[Glucose Transport]
    B --> C[Glucose Inside Cell]
    
    %% Step 1: Hexokinase
    C --> D{Glucose Available?}
    D -->|Yes| E[Hexokinase II]
    D -->|No| DEND[No Glucose Available - Pathway Inactive]
    E --> F[Glucose + ATP]
    F --> G[Phosphorylation]
    G --> H[Glucose-6-Phosphate]
    
    %% Step 2: Phosphoglucose Isomerase
    H --> I[Phosphoglucose Isomerase]
    I --> J[Isomerization]
    J --> K[Fructose-6-Phosphate]
    
    %% Step 3: Phosphofructokinase (KEY CONTROL)
    K --> L[Phosphofructokinase]
    L --> M{ATP Available?}
    M -->|Yes| N[F6P + ATP]
    M -->|No| MEND[/Insufficient ATP - Glycolysis Halted/]
    N --> O[Phosphorylation]
    O --> P[Fructose-1,6-Bisphosphate]
    
    %% Allosteric Regulation
    Q[High ATP] --> R[/PFK Inhibition/]
    S[High AMP] --> T[PFK Activation]
    R --> L
    T --> L
    
    %% Step 4: Aldolase
    P --> U(\1)
    U --> V(\1)
    V --> W[DHAP + G3P]
    
    %% Step 5: Triose Phosphate Isomerase
    W --> X[Triose-P Isomerase]
    X --> Y[DHAP to G3P]
    Y --> Z[2 x G3P]
    
    %% Step 6: G3P Dehydrogenase (ENERGY CAPTURE)
    Z --> AA[G3P Dehydrogenase]
    AA --> AB{NAD+ Available?}
    AB -->|Yes| AC[G3P + NAD+ + Pi]
    AB -->|No| ABEND[/No NAD+ Available - Oxidation Blocked/]
    AC --> AD[Oxidation + Phosphorylation]
    AD --> AE[1,3-Bisphosphoglycerate]
    AD --> AF[NADH Produced]
    
    %% Step 7: Phosphoglycerate Kinase (ATP PRODUCTION)
    AE --> AG[Phosphoglycerate Kinase]
    AG --> AH[Substrate-Level Phosphorylation]
    AH --> AI[3-Phosphoglycerate]
    AH --> AJ[ATP Produced]
    
    %% Step 8: Phosphoglycerate Mutase
    AI --> AK[Phosphoglycerate Mutase]
    AK --> AL[Isomerization]
    AL --> AM[2-Phosphoglycerate]
    
    %% Step 9: Enolase
    AM --> AN(\1)
    AN --> AO(\1)
    AO --> AP[Phosphoenolpyruvate]
    
    %% Step 10: Pyruvate Kinase (ATP PRODUCTION)
    AP --> AQ[Pyruvate Kinase]
    AQ --> AR[Substrate-Level Phosphorylation]
    AR --> AS(\1)
    AR --> AT[ATP Produced]
    
    %% Products and Fates
    AS --> AU{Oxygen Available?}
    AU -->|Yes| AV[Aerobic Respiration]
    AU -->|No| AW(\1)
    
    %% Aerobic Path
    AV --> AX[Pyruvate to Mitochondria]
    AX --> AY[TCA Cycle Entry]
    AY --> AZ[Complete Oxidation]
    AZ --> BA[36-38 ATP Total]
    
    %% Anaerobic Path
    AW --> BB[Pyruvate Decarboxylase]
    BB --> BC(\1)
    BC --> BD[Alcohol Dehydrogenase]
    BD --> BE[Ethanol Production]
    BD --> BF[NAD+ Regeneration]
    BF --> BG[Glycolysis Continues]
    
    %% Energy Balance
    AF --> BH[2 NADH]
    AJ --> BI[2 ATP]
    AT --> BJ[2 ATP]
    BI --> BK["Gross: 4 ATP"]
    F --> BL["Cost: 2 ATP"]
    BK --> BM["Net: 2 ATP"]
    
    %% Styling
    
    
    
    
    

    %% Semantic Color Styling

    %% Semantic Color Styling

    %% Semantic Color Styling

    %% Semantic Color Styling
    style A fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style B fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style C fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

name: Glycolysis and Glucose Metabolism
notes: This flowchart represents glycolysis with 56 unique nodes (A-BM). Contains 4 OR logic gates (glucose check, ATP check, NAD+ check, oxygen check). Demonstrates the 10-step enzymatic pathway with explicit ATP investment (2), ATP production (4), and NADH generation (2). Shows both aerobic (TCA cycle) and anaerobic (ethanol fermentation) fates of pyruvate. PFK is highlighted as the rate-limiting step with allosteric regulation.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on verified biochemical data from decades of glycolysis research. All 10 enzymatic steps, regulatory mechanisms, and energy balances have been validated against primary research sources and biochemistry textbooks. The pathway represents the canonical Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas pathway with yeast-specific regulatory features including glucose repression and ethanol fermentation coupling.

sources.journal: Progress in Biophysics and Molecular Biology
sources.note: Comprehensive review of glycolysis evolution and regulation
sources.title: Evolution of glycolysis
The molecular genetics of hexose transport in yeasts

sources.authors: Diaz-Ruiz R, Rigoulet M, Devin A
sources.note: Modern understanding of glycolysis regulation in yeast
sources.title: The Warburg and Crabtree effects: On the origin of cancer cell energy metabolism and of yeast glucose repression
